facts.shortSummary: After killing the dragon, the party ships off valuable remains with Vahaiya, folds a band of kobolds into Asineau's life, and expands a growing map of nearby fey realms.
facts.longSummary: The session opens in the aftermath of the dragon kill as Izgil sorts out useful dragon parts, Vahaiya carries the marketable remains back to elven lands for eventual repayment in crafted magic, and the party settles into its new role as lords of Asineau. Robin's effort to make the village more open and prosperous now includes kobold retainers and stronger ties with the local lizardfolk, while Celyn and Izgil turn their attention to a large annotated map of the Feywild and its mortal correspondences. Their work draws together what they know about Ethlenn's lands, Lord Umbraeth's reach, Greymalkin's lost realm, and new rumors from western Addermarch about another destroyed portal.

location.context: the village after the dragon fight, including the party's household and Celyn's growing fey-realm map project
location.notes: The whole beat stays in post-dragon village life and planning rather than moving to a new adventure site.

npcs.name: Vahaiya
npcs.context: elven ally carrying the dragon remains home
npcs.notes: They take the marketable dragon parts to elven crafters who are expected to return equivalent magical gifts.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.context: wingless gryphon now venerated by the kobolds
npcs.notes: His past and grudges also inform the party's growing map of fey realms.

npcs.name: Ethlenn
npcs.context: fey queen whose territory anchors the party's map
npcs.notes: Celyn and Izgil are especially clear on the bounds of her realm and its tie to Tyrwingha.

npcs.name: Lord Umbraeth
npcs.context: the Gloomshaper whose claimed territory shapes the party's planning
npcs.notes: His expansion in the Feywild is one of the main problems Celyn and Izgil are trying to understand.

items.name: dragon remains
items.notes: Izgil sorts the carcass into useful magical components and marketable trophies.

items.name: annotated fey-realm map
items.notes: Celyn and Izgil begin compiling a heavily marked map of fey realms and their mortal counterparts.

facts.shortSummary: Royal retainers inspect Asineau for Queen Elaine II's visit, and the party turns the hosting problem into a plan to escort her through the swamps themselves.
facts.longSummary: Early in October, a royal advance party arrives in Asineau to see whether the village can host the queen for a single night during her circuit through Aveil. Robin, Celyn, and the others work through sleeping arrangements, kitchens, and the awkward realities of entertaining an entourage from a poor but newly important village, while Celyn gets a small private satisfaction out of being able to produce Tyrwinghan wine from home. By the end of the discussion the real concern is security, so the party decides to ride out ahead of the queen, camp beyond the swamps, and serve as her escort on the dangerous stretch of road.

location.context: the village during the royal advance visit and final escort planning
location.notes: The beat never leaves Asineau; it is entirely about preparations for the queen's arrival.

npcs.name: Elaine II
npcs.context: queen whose circuit will bring her to Asineau
npcs.notes: The village is judged fit for a one-night royal stop before she moves on to Cleenseau.

items.name: Tyrwinghan wine
items.notes: Celyn produces wine from his home village as part of the hosting preparations.

facts.shortSummary: While escorting the queen's train through the swamps, the party recognizes the Hunter's signature work in red-eyed elk and braces for an ambush on the road.
facts.longSummary: The escort begins with the party's kobolds scouting ahead of the baggage train until they spot giant deer charging in with the same red-eyed corruption the Hunter used before. The warning confirms that the attack the party feared is underway, so Robin moves to shield the entourage, Viepuck links minds with him, Izgil tracks the Hunter's spear, and Celyn slips into cover to tag the attacker with magic if he can. The opening exchange ends when the Hunter commits to the fight and teleports into melee after throwing his spear at the guards.

location.primary: swamp road between Asineau and Cleenseau
location.context: the queen's baggage train and escort column moving through the dangerous marsh approach
location.notes: The beat stays on the road where the ambush first breaks open.

npcs.name: Elaine II
npcs.context: queen traveling with the entourage under the party's protection
npcs.notes: The escort is organized specifically to get her safely through the dangerous stretch of Aveil road.

npcs.name: The Hunter
npcs.notes: The red-eyed elk and the tracked spear confirm that he is lying in wait for the queen's passage.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.context: Robin's gryphon shouting a challenge into the woods
npcs.notes: He openly taunts the Hunter as the ambush takes shape.

items.name: Hunter's spear
items.notes: Izgil locates the spear to confirm that the Hunter is in position before the melee begins.

mainEnemies.name: The Hunter
mainEnemies.notes: The main attacker lying in wait for the queen's escort.

mainEnemies.name: ensorcelled elk
mainEnemies.notes: The red-eyed beasts are used to open the ambush and scatter the train.

combat.notes: The first phase of the fight is about recognizing the trap, protecting the entourage, and surviving the Hunter's first committed strike.

facts.shortSummary: A Duskhound reaches the entourage and seizes Queen Elaine II, but Viepuck and Izgil improvise a vertical rescue while the party beats the Hunter to death.
facts.longSummary: The ambush escalates when a Duskhound teleports into the royal ring and grabs the queen after the Hunter has already broken into the guard. Robin keeps pressure on the Hunter despite a spear wound, the guards try to keep the lesser threat contained, and the whole fight suddenly turns on whether the queen can be pulled clear before the Duskhound escapes with her. Viepuck solves that problem with startling audacity by having Es*tiasilos deliver Fly, then Izgil follows up with Dimension Door straight upward so the queen is hauled out of the melee and away from the extraction attempt. Deprived of his prize and roaring orders to warn Lord Umbraeth, the Hunter is then cut down on the road.

location.primary: swamp road between Asineau and Cleenseau
location.context: the royal guard's defensive ring around the queen as the ambush becomes a rescue scene
location.notes: The fight remains centered on the baggage train and the queen's immediate surroundings.

npcs.name: Elaine II
npcs.context: queen seized and then rescued from the Duskhound
npcs.notes: The entire beat turns on keeping her from being abducted into the Feywild.

npcs.name: The Hunter
npcs.context: fey ambusher fighting inside the royal guard's formation
npcs.notes: He drives the attack until the party kills him at the roadside.

npcs.name: Duskhound
npcs.context: teleporting fey hound attempting the abduction
npcs.notes: It grabs the queen and nearly escapes before Izgil blocks the extraction.

npcs.name: Es*tiasilos
npcs.context: Viepuck's familiar carrying the rescue spell into the melee
npcs.notes: It delivers the Fly spell that lets the queen be lifted clear.

items.name: Hunter's spear
items.notes: The Hunter uses the spear to teleport into the guard ring and later leaves it buried painfully in Robin's side.

mainEnemies.name: The Hunter
mainEnemies.notes: He remains the central battlefield threat until the party finally kills him.

mainEnemies.name: Duskhound
mainEnemies.notes: The hound's successful grab of the queen forces the fight into an immediate rescue operation.

combat.notes: This phase is defined by the queen's near-abduction, the desperate spell rescue, and the Hunter's death.

facts.shortSummary: The surviving Duskhound tries to flee and warn Lord Umbraeth, but Robin, Celyn, Greymalkin, and Viepuck run it down before it can escape.
facts.longSummary: With the Hunter dead, the last open question is whether the Duskhound will get away and carry a report back through the Gloomshaper's network. Robin and Greymalkin launch into pursuit at magical speed, sweep Celyn into the chase, and keep pressure on the hound while Izgil falls more slowly back toward the field and Viepuck works around the still-flying queen problem. The chase stays just close enough for repeated blows until Celyn finally kills the fleeing Duskhound, after which the party regroups and rapidly assesses how much danger remains if Lord Umbraeth notices the silence.

location.primary: swamp road between Asineau and Cleenseau
location.context: the running pursuit away from the baggage train and the regroup immediately afterward
location.notes: Although the action spreads out, the chase remains tied to the same escort road and ends back in discussion at the ambush site.

npcs.name: Elaine II
npcs.context: queen kept aloft and protected while the chase finishes
npcs.notes: The party is still managing her safety while it hunts down the last enemy.

npcs.name: Duskhound
npcs.context: last surviving attacker fleeing with orders to warn Lord Umbraeth
npcs.notes: The party kills it before it can escape the road or deliver the message.

npcs.name: Greymalkin
npcs.context: mount and pursuit engine for Robin and Celyn
npcs.notes: His magically accelerated speed is what makes the kill possible.

npcs.name: Lord Umbraeth
npcs.context: fey lord the Duskhound is trying to warn
npcs.notes: The regrouping conversation centers on how quickly he may notice the hound's death.

mainEnemies.name: Duskhound
mainEnemies.notes: The final hostile survivor of the ambush.

combat.notes: The last combat phase is a fast moving chase whose entire purpose is to prevent a warning from reaching Lord Umbraeth.

facts.shortSummary: After the ambush, the party tells Queen Elaine II that the only real answer is to close the Feywild portal, and she orders them to do it at once.
facts.longSummary: Once the party has a moment to breathe, it explains to the queen that the roadside victory has not really ended the problem so long as the Feywild portal remains open and useful to Lord Umbraeth's servants. Celyn states plainly that permanently closing the portal is the only practical way to thwart the threat for any meaningful length of time, while Robin makes no effort to hide how tired he is of fey disasters. Elaine II accepts the answer immediately and asks them to undertake the job, so the new plan becomes getting her safely into Cleenseau, stopping briefly in Asineau, gathering what little more they can from their research, sleeping, and striking the portal early the next morning.

location.primary: swamp road between Asineau and Cleenseau
location.context: the roadside regroup and strategy discussion immediately after the ambush
location.notes: The beat stays in the aftermath conversation before the party rides on toward Cleenseau.

npcs.name: Elaine II
npcs.context: queen taking counsel and then issuing the order to close the portal
npcs.notes: She accepts the risk assessment and puts the next mission directly on the party.

npcs.name: Lord Umbraeth
npcs.context: archfey whose network still threatens Aveil while the portal remains active
npcs.notes: The discussion assumes that only shutting the portal will seriously blunt his reach into the region.
